scenes.text: Most people think one drink helps them sleep.
It may help you fall asleep faster, but that is not the same thing as sleeping well.
Alcohol suppresses REM and disrupts the second half of the night when your brain should be recovering.
Even low doses change sleep architecture, so the effect starts earlier than most people realize.
If you care about sleep quality, leave more time between drinking and bed. Follow BestKarma for science-backed health without the hype.
